If you value your time, go elsewhere.", "Please read.
When looking for a plumber, there are two types of businesses: The first is the small family-owned type operation that employs mostly the same reliable people from year to year. The second is a big operation where you recognize the name through heavy advertising on the sides of buses and a large fleet of service vans. The former works on repeat business and customer loyalty. They stay small to so that the business is built on reliability and relationships. The latter is built on high volume and very high pricing. They do not care about customer loyalty. They want that highly profitable big sale from people who are either desperate or who don't know any better. And the pricing is outrageous and deceptive. The inspection fee is reasonable and then if you agree, they really get you. Make no mistake about it. It is YOU who is paying for all that advertising on the sides of buses, not them.
I have been using Soreano' Plumbing for about 25 years. This is a small family-type operation that has employed mostly the same good people for years. Repeat customers who have been with them a long time are usually given quick service. The work is not cheap but then, I have never had a case where it was not done correctly. As a precaution, I would  note that you should be very specific about what the problem is what you want done before they come out so they bring the right tools or equipment. Otherwise, I recommend them without reservation."]

I was impressed from the time of the on-site quoted estimate , because David thoroughly scoped and  inspected my pipes, took the time to carefully unscrew and the replace my deck boards as he proceeded to inspect for every possible clean out pipe available before giving me a quote. David explained the problem, what the work to fix the problem would look like and how long it would take to resolve.
